messages.content: Plan 5 days in Rome with $2000 budget.
Actually, let's change to Paris instead.

Perfect — Paris is an excellent choice for vegan travelers. Here are a few spots that match your preference:

- Louvre Museum — Right in the center with tons of vegan‑friendly cafés and plant‑based lunch spots within easy walking distance, so you can pair art-filled mornings with delicious vegan meals.  
- Eiffel Tower / Champ de Mars — Great for a relaxed vegan picnic on the lawns with many nearby bakeries and casual restaurants offering plant‑based options.  
- Notre Dame / Latin Quarter — A lively area full of small bistros and market streets where vegetarian and vegan dishes are common, ideal for wandering and tasting local plant‑based fare.

Which one would you like to explore more — I can pull restaurant recommendations, tours, or a sample itinerary — or would you like me to refine the search to a particular neighborhood (like Le Marais or the Latin Quarter)?

itinerary.name: The best of Rome running tour
itinerary.shortDescription: Dawn running tour from the Spanish Steps to Trevi Fountain, Pantheon, Piazza Navona and hidden spots (ArcheoRunning style).

itinerary.name: Colosseum Underground & Roman Forum Guided Tour
itinerary.shortDescription: Semi-private guided visit including the Colosseum subterranean levels, arena, and the Roman Forum.

itinerary.name: The Unconventional Ancient Rome (private)
itinerary.shortDescription: Private/custom tour: walk on the Colosseum arena, Palatine Hill, House of Augustus, Roman Forum, Imperial Forums — itinerary customizable.

itinerary.name: Galleria Borghese (guided) / Borghese Gallery MUSEMENT (skip-the-line option)
itinerary.shortDescription: Visit the Borghese Collection (Bernini, Caravaggio). Choose guided visit (€75) or cheaper skip-the-line option (€6.61).

itinerary.name: Tour Pompeii from Rome - Group Tour
itinerary.shortDescription: Full-day guided/travel day to Pompeii from Rome. Available Wed/Fri/Sun, approx 07:30–16:30/17:00.

itinerary.name: Colonna Gallery Entrance Tickets
itinerary.shortDescription: Baroque Galleria Colonna — great for a short museum stop (Ceiling frescoes and large Baroque rooms).

user_query: Actually, let's change to Paris instead.

steps.prompt: Plan 5 days in Rome with $2000 budget.
steps.response: Routing to Trip_Planner: User gave a clear destination (Rome), duration (5 days) and budget ($2000), so we can build the trip.

steps.prompt: Plan a 5-day Rome itinerary on $2000 for 1 traveler (vegan).

steps.response:
[
  {
    "name": "Rome",
    "section": "by bicycle",
    "description": "There is the possibility to hire any kind of bike in Rome: from tandem, road bikes, children bikes to trekking bikes. Some shops are even specialised only on high quality ones while street stands will hire you cheaper and heavy ones. Bicycling alone can be stressful because of the traffic. The best way is to discover first how to move around and avoid traffic and stress with a guide thanks to one ",
    "source": "Wikivoyage",
    "score": 0.45581463
  },
  {
    "name": "Rome",
    "section": "learn",
    "description": "Rome is replete with foreign language and cultural institutions. Of course, learning Italian is a worthwhile activity if you plan to stay for any length of time. If you plan to combine a stay in Rome with academic study, there are several English-language universities. * The regional government and two historical societies are offering '''[mailto:ilregnodicamelot@libero.it free Latin classes]''

I’ve put together a 5‑day, attractions-focused Rome plan that fits your $2,000 budget and one traveler. Below is a compact day-by-day itinerary using the bookable activities we found, plus quick practical notes (vegan food tips, Roma Pass, & booking links). If you want me to reserve any specific tour, change the order, or add exact dates, tell me your origin city and preferred travel dates and I’ll update and book where possible.

Quick highlights:
- Duration: 5 days in Rome
- Budget: $2,000 (USD) — activities below show prices in EUR as provided
- Travelers: 1

Suggested 5-day plan (activities from the search results):
Day 1 — Morning: The best of Rome running tour (discover Spanish Steps, Trevi Fountain, Pantheon, Piazza Navona at dawn)
  • Price: €110.00
  • Booking: https://eu5.bookingkit.de/checkout2/addToCart/?event_id=319922e96da29d83bb5857e455b19b35&sid=noCart&ref=iisTdWCzXj7nb8C
Day 1 — Afternoon / Optional: Walk central sights on foot (use suggestions from Wikivoyage: on foot is best)

Day 2 — Colosseum-focused
  • Colosseum Underground & Roman Forum Guided Tour — includes subterranean levels, arena, Roman Forum
  • Price: €149.00
  • Booking: https://eu5.bookingkit.de/checkout2/addToCart/?event_id=b313c3eabec6226b3b27d36082b83795&sid=noCart&ref=iisTdWCzXj7nb8C

Day 3 — Classical Rome & Palatine
  • The Unconventional Ancient Rome (private) — arena walking on the Colosseum, Palatine Hill, Roman Forum and Imperial Forums; itinerary customizable. (Optional/private)
  • Price listed: €0.00 (custom pricing usually applies) 
  • Booking: https://eu5.bookingkit.de/checkout2/addToCart/?event_id=602598d9f130d6794dfb6252b932fd04&sid=noCart&ref=iisTdWCzXj7nb8C

Day 4 — Museums & Galleries
  • Galleria Borghese guided visit (Spanish or Italian options available). Price shown: €75.00 for guided ticket.
  • Or the lower-cost Borghese Gallery MUSEMENT skip-the-line option (€6.61) if you prefer cheaper entry and independent exploration.
  • Bookings (where available): MUSEMENT option — https://eu5.bookingkit.de/checkout2/addToCart/?event_id=7b0b4ede317f489049a04a0e97bb4280&sid=noCart&ref=iisTdWCzXj7nb8C

Day 5 — Day trip to Pompeii
  • Tour Pompeii from Rome - Group Tour (available Wed/Fri/Sun, about 07:30–16:30/17:00) — Price: €137.00 — booking link: https://amadeus-sherpa-es.regiondo.com/tour-da-roma-per-pompei-in-bus?vendor=38759
  • Alternative (from results): “Da Roma: tour giornaliero a Pompei” (voucher skip-the-line, transfer included) — Price: €220.00 — booking link: https://eu5.bookingkit.de/checkout2/addToCart/?event_id=781953c9b2ed4aaeee1e730f457ce58e&sid=noCart&ref=iisTdWCzXj7nb8C

Add-ons / Minor sights:
  • Colonna Gallery entrance tickets — €19.50 (good one-hour stop for Baroque art)
  • Practical tip: consider a Roma Pass (3-day) — it can save on transport + first 2 museum/site admissions (Wikivoyage note: approx €58.50 as of Aug 2024).

Vegan traveler notes (since vegan is an interest):
  • Rome has many vegan and vegan-friendly restaurants — Trastevere, Monti, and near Termini/City Centre have good options. I can list top vegan restaurants close to each day’s activities.
  • When booking tours with included food/coffee, ask in advance for vegan options.

Next steps I can take for you (pick one):
  • Add exact travel dates and I’ll check availability and move activities to specific days (I’ll need your origin city for any transport/reservations).
  • Book any of the activities above (I’ll confirm availability and payment links).
  • Replace any day with alternative suggestions (food tour, Vatican visit, underground crypts, nightlife).

If you’re happy with this attractions-only draft, tell me which activities to book (and provide origin city + dates if you want me to check transport or availability).

APPROVE: Good 5‑day attractions draft — fits the 5‑day requirement and stays well under the $2,000 budget. Two quick fixes before booking: confirm the private “Unconventional Ancient Rome” price (€0 looks like a placeholder) and consider whether you want two Colosseum experiences on Days 2–3 (they overlap; you could combine or swap one for the Vatican/crypts). Also check Pompeii tour weekday availability (runs Wed/Fri/Sun) and confirm vegan options with any tours that include food.
